binaries.title: ndis.sys - WIL feature-staged miniport/interface synchronization, old path retained

verdict: No security-relevant change is delivered unconditionally by this build. The patched binary adds two WIL feature-staging gates (Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix at 0x140092D98, Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 at 0x1400A4B60) and, behind them, stages new synchronization behavior in the miniport check-for-hang/wake-up DPC path and the NDIS interface-block create/delete lifecycle. In every affected function the pre-existing code path is fully retained and runs unchanged when the feature resolves disabled. This is a staged rollout, not a delivered fix.

changed_functions.name: ndisMWakeUpDpcX
key_changes: Added Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline() gate at 0x14007ACF9
Feature-ON branch (0x14007AD10): acquires the +0x60 spinlock, then calls KeSetEvent(block+0x778) while holding it (0x14007AD41), releasing after (0x14007ADB9)
Feature-OFF branch (0x14007AD54): retains the original behavior, byte-equivalent to unpatched (KeSetEvent at 0x14007AD65 with lock released, re-acquired at 0x14007AD7A)
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched calls KeSetEvent(block+0x778) after KeReleaseSpinLockFromDpcLevel (early-exit path 0x14007CBE9->0x14007CC01; deferred path releases at 0x14007CAFB, signals at 0x14007CB79). The patch stages an alternative that signals under the +0x60 spinlock, but only when Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix resolves enabled; otherwise the original path runs. Not a delivered change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (ndisMWakeUpDpcX @ 0x14007CA70):
0x14007CBE9: call KeReleaseSpinLockFromDpcLevel
0x14007CBF5: lea rcx,[rbx+778h]
0x14007CC01: call KeSetEvent            ; lock not held
0x14007CB79: call KeSetEvent            ; deferred path, lock not held
0x14007CB89: call KeAcquireSpinLockAtDpcLevel ; re-acquire afterward

PATCHED (ndisMWakeUpDpcX @ 0x14007ABE0):
0x14007ACF9: call Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14007AD00: jz loc_14007AD54           ; feature OFF -> original path
0x14007AD14: call KeAcquireSpinLockAtDpcLevel ; ON: acquire first
0x14007AD41: call KeSetEvent            ; ON: under spinlock
0x14007ADB9: call KeReleaseSpinLockFromDpcLevel
0x14007AD65: call KeSetEvent            ; OFF: lock not held (== unpatched)
0x14007AD7A: call KeAcquireSpinLockAtDpcLevel

changed_functions.name: ndisQueuedCheckForHang
key_changes: Added Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix gate at 0x14007B0B5
Feature-ON branch (0x14007B197): acquires +0x60 spinlock, calls KeSetEvent(rdi+0x778) under it (0x14007B1C6), releases after (0x14007B207)
Feature-OFF branch (0x14007B0C2): original behavior retained, matches unpatched KeSetEvent at 0x14007CF01 with lock not held
changed_functions.security_notes: Unpatched signals rdi+0x778 at 0x14007CF01 with the +0x60 spinlock not held (released earlier in the loop). The patch stages a signal-under-spinlock variant behind Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix; the original path is retained when the feature is disabled. Not a delivered change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (ndisQueuedCheckForHang @ 0x14007CC60):
0x14007CEF5: lea rcx,[rdi+778h]
0x14007CF01: call KeSetEvent            ; lock not held

PATCHED (ndisQueuedCheckForHang @ 0x14007AE20):
0x14007B0B5: call Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14007B0BC: jnz loc_14007B197          ; feature ON -> signal under spinlock
0x14007B0CE: call KeSetEvent            ; OFF: lock not held (== unpatched)
0x14007B197: call KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc ; ON: acquire first
0x14007B1C6: call KeSetEvent            ; ON: under spinlock
0x14007B207: call KeReleaseSpinLock

changed_functions.name: ndisIfRegisterInterfaceEx
key_changes: Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 gate added
Feature-ON branch adds the PendingDeletionComplete KEVENT wait handshake so registration waits for a still-pending interface deletion before proceeding
Feature-OFF branch retains the original interface-list lookup under the existing spinlock (WPP_MAIN_CB.Reserved)
changed_functions.security_notes: Interface registration path. The new event-based wait-for-deletion handshake is gated by Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 with the original path retained. Not a delivered change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Function is large; the delivered delta is the Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 gate plus the PendingDeletionComplete wait, with the original lookup path retained when the feature is off.

changed_functions.name: ndisIfCreateInterfaceFromPersistentStore
key_changes: Added Feature_NDPQualitySummer26__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline() gate (feature==0 runs the original path)

Feature-ON branch: KeInitializeEvent(&Event, NotificationEvent, 0); if the matching block is pending deletion, parks &Event in block->PendingDeletionComplete and waits via ndisWaitForKernelObject, then re-verifies (block->PendingDeletionComplete == &Event) before proceeding
Interface list is protected by the same spinlock (WPP_MAIN_CB.Reserved) in both branches; a secondary pending-deletion list is used only on the gated path
changed_functions.security_notes: Reached from ndisLoadNetworkInterfaceFromPersistedState (reconstructing interface state from persisted/registry data during interface initialization), not from an OID/IOCTL request. It looks up NDIS interface blocks by NetLuid/GUID (ndisIfFindInterfaceByNetLuid / ndisIfFindNetworkBlock) and creates them via ndisIfCreateInterface (0x1401676D0). The staged handshake serializes creation against a pending deletion of the same interface. Old path retained when the feature is off. Not a delivered change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: PATCHED entry (0x140089D40): if (Feature_NDPQualitySummer26__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline() == 0) { original lookup under WPP_MAIN_CB.Reserved } else { KeInitializeEvent + park &Event in block->PendingDeletionComplete + ndisWaitForKernelObject + re-verify }.

changed_functions.name: IFBLOCK_DECREMENT_REF
key_changes: Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 gate added around a new event signal
Feature-ON: signals block->PendingDeletionComplete before ExFreePoolWithTag(block, 0)
Feature-OFF: no new event signal; the free path is otherwise unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: Interface-block reference decrementer that frees the block at refcount zero. The patch adds signalling of the new PendingDeletionComplete handshake event before the free, gated by Feature_NDPQualitySummer26. Not a delivered change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: PATCHED: if (block->AsyncEvent) KeSetEvent(block->AsyncEvent,0,0); if (Feature_NDPQualitySummer26...()) { if (block->PendingDeletionComplete) KeSetEvent(block->PendingDeletionComplete,0,0); } ExFreePoolWithTag(block,0);

changed_functions.name: ndisNsiEnumerateAllInterfaceInformation
key_changes: Interface-list traversal adjusted alongside the same interface-lifecycle work
No delivered behavior change with the feature off
changed_functions.security_notes: NSI (Network Store Interface) enumerate-all query path. Not the 'bind/unbind handler' or reference-counting fix; the changes track the staged interface-lifecycle reorganization.

changed_functions.name: ndisNsiGetInterfaceInformation
key_changes: Variable renaming and control-flow reshaping alongside the interface-lifecycle work
Interface-list access paths adjusted
changed_functions.security_notes: NSI get-interface-information query path. Not impersonation-token handling. Tracks the same interface-lifecycle reorganization; no delivered security change.

changed_functions.name: ndisNsiGetAllInterfaceInformation
key_changes: Interface-list access paths adjusted alongside the interface-lifecycle work
changed_functions.security_notes: NSI get-all-interface-information query path. Tracks the same reorganization; no delivered security change.

changed_functions.name: ndisIfInitializePhase1
key_changes: Initialization of the additional interface list used by the gated PendingDeletionComplete handshake
Reordered initialization sequence
changed_functions.security_notes: Initialization support for the staged interface-lifecycle handshake.

changed_functions.name: Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
key_changes: New WIL feature helper: returns featureState&1 when featureState&0x10, else the compiled fallback
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staging gate for the DPC/check-for-hang change.

changed_functions.name: Feature_NDPQualitySummer26__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
key_changes: New WIL feature helper gating the interface-block create/delete handshake
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staging gate for the interface-lifecycle change.

changed_functions.name: NdisWdfRegisterCx
key_changes: Expected structure size 0x78->0x80
IoAllocateDriverObjectExtension size 0xA0->0xA8, matching memset 0xA0->0xA8
changed_functions.security_notes: Structure-size increase for new fields, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: ndisWdfNotifyStuckOperation
key_changes: New telemetry/notification helper (_NDIS_CX_STUCK_OPERATION_TYPE)
changed_functions.security_notes: Watchdog/telemetry notification, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: WPP_RECORDER_SF_* tracing helpers and init reordering
key_changes: WPP/ETW tracing thunks, telemetry IDs, and initialization reordering supporting the staged features

security_findings.entry_point: Internal miniport check-for-hang timer / wake-up DPC and adapter halt/reset sequencing (not an unprivileged user-mode request)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Race/ordering hardening (adjacent to CWE-362); no demonstrable use-after-free primitive in these binaries
security_findings.description: In the unpatched build, ndisMWakeUpDpcX and ndisQueuedCheckForHang signal the miniport block's embedded event at +0x778 (KeSetEvent) after releasing the +0x60 spinlock, and re-acquire it afterward (ndisMWakeUpDpcX early-exit 0x14007CBE9->0x14007CC01 and deferred 0x14007CB79->0x14007CB89; ndisQueuedCheckForHang 0x14007CF01). The patched build adds a Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix gate (0x14007ACF9 / 0x14007B0B5); when enabled it signals the event while holding the +0x60 spinlock. When disabled, the original lock-released-then-signal path runs unchanged. No behavior change is delivered unconditionally.
security_findings.reachability: These callbacks are internal to the miniport lifecycle (check-for-hang timer, wake-up DPC, reset). No attacker-controlled user-mode trigger for the teardown race is evidenced in this corpus. The miniport block is reference-counted (ndisReferenceMiniport/ndisDereferenceMiniport) around the work.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The embedded event at +0x778 is real; no pool tag, allocation size, or object-replacement path is evidenced. Whether the original ordering is an exploitable use-after-free or a benign timing hardening is not determinable from these binaries.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED ndisMWakeUpDpcX @ 0x14007CA70:
0x14007CBE9 call KeReleaseSpinLockFromDpcLevel
0x14007CC01 call KeSetEvent            ; [rbx+778h], lock not held
0x14007CB79 call KeSetEvent            ; deferred path, lock not held
0x14007CB89 call KeAcquireSpinLockAtDpcLevel

PATCHED ndisMWakeUpDpcX @ 0x14007ABE0:
0x14007ACF9 call Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14007AD00 jz loc_14007AD54           ; OFF -> original path
0x14007AD14 call KeAcquireSpinLockAtDpcLevel ; ON: acquire first
0x14007AD41 call KeSetEvent            ; ON: under spinlock
0x14007ADB9 call KeReleaseSpinLockFromDpcLevel
0x14007AD65 call KeSetEvent            ; OFF: lock not held (== unpatched)

UNPATCHED ndisQueuedCheckForHang @ 0x14007CC60:
0x14007CEF5 lea rcx,[rdi+778h]
0x14007CF01 call KeSetEvent            ; lock not held

PATCHED ndisQueuedCheckForHang @ 0x14007AE20:
0x14007B0B5 call Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14007B0BC jnz loc_14007B197          ; ON -> under spinlock
0x14007B0CE call KeSetEvent            ; OFF: lock not held (== unpatched)
0x14007B1C6 call KeSetEvent            ; ON: under spinlock (0x14007B197 block)

security_findings.entry_point: Interface initialization from persisted (registry) state via ndisLoadNetworkInterfaceFromPersistedState, and interface registration; not an unprivileged user-mode OID/IOCTL request
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Create/delete serialization hardening (adjacent to CWE-362); no demonstrable primitive in these binaries
security_findings.description: The patched build adds a Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 gate around a new PendingDeletionComplete KEVENT handshake in the interface-block (_NDIS_IF_BLOCK) lifecycle: interface-create paths park a local event in block->PendingDeletionComplete and wait (ndisWaitForKernelObject) when a matching block is still pending deletion, and IFBLOCK_DECREMENT_REF signals that event before ExFreePoolWithTag. When the feature is disabled, the original list lookup and free run unchanged. No behavior change is delivered unconditionally.
security_findings.reachability: ndisIfCreateInterfaceFromPersistentStore is reached from ndisLoadNetworkInterfaceFromPersistedState (interface init from registry). The interface list is protected by the same spinlock (WPP_MAIN_CB.Reserved) in both builds and branches.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. sub_1401676D0 is ndisIfCreateInterface (interface creation), not OID processing. No pool tag or allocation size for a use-after-free is evidenced.

summary: This ndis.sys diff delivers no unconditional security-relevant change. The patched build introduces two WIL feature-staging gates (Feature_CFHCancelTimerFix at 0x140092D98; Feature_NDPQualitySummer26 at 0x1400A4B60) and stages behind them: (1) signalling the miniport block's embedded event at +0x778 under the +0x60 spinlock in ndisMWakeUpDpcX / ndisQueuedCheckForHang instead of after releasing it, and (2) a PendingDeletionComplete KEVENT handshake serializing NDIS interface-block creation against pending deletion in ndisIfCreateInterfaceFromPersistentStore / ndisIfRegisterInterfaceEx / IFBLOCK_DECREMENT_REF. In every case the original path is fully retained and runs when the feature resolves disabled, so the change is a staged rollout rather than a delivered fix. The remaining changed functions are NSI query paths, initialization support for the new list, WIL feature helpers, telemetry/tracing (WPP), a NdisWdfRegisterCx structure-size bump, and a new watchdog notification helper. The earlier characterization of these as delivered unprivileged-user-mode OID use-after-free vulnerabilities with pool-spray/code-execution primitives is not supported by the binaries: the affected functions are internal miniport and interface-lifecycle routines, the entry points are timer/DPC and registry-persisted interface init rather than IOCTL/OID, sub_1401676D0 is ndisIfCreateInterface (not OID processing), and no pool tag, allocation size, or use-after-free primitive is present in the disassembly.
